current_status: Formal project F2 autoload matrix is established and F3 DataLoader schema closure has started. DataLoader now exposes validate_project_data() and schema_counts(); client boot smoke calls the schema validator and logs data_schema_ok plus counts for player stats, locale keys, growth levels, growth pools, meta upgrade tracks, and meta unlocks. DataLoader and tools/validate_data.py currently validate player.json, meta_progression.json, client/locale/strings.csv, growth.csv, and growth_pools.json with fail-fast file + field diagnostics. client/data/growth.csv and client/data/growth_pools.json now provide the first growth curve and level-up pool golden examples without implementing GrowthSystem, upgrade UI, or mode runtime. MVP code remains isolated. The next F3 priority is to add the future game_modes.json boundary for resource pools, weights, blocklists, lightweight overrides, participant/team fields, and then add DataLoader L1 coverage when the test runner is introduced.
last_completed: AI knowledge base v2 with docs_health_check
Experience and level-up choice system decision (ADR #43)
AI memory split into long-term index, current_state.json, and session logs (ADR #44)
MVP M4.6 centralized player, input, weapon, enemy, spawner, and background tunables in MinimumViableProduct/client/data/mvp_config.json
Full project numeric and localization configuration manuals established in client/data/README.md and client/locale/README.md (ADR #45)
Full project deep meta progression specified with meta currency, account level, permanent upgrades, unlocks, and SaveManager meta persistence boundary (ADR #46)
Human-readable future task backlog established in docs/TODO.md
Minimal Stage 1 CI enabled in .github/workflows/docs-check.yml for JSON, CSV, docs health, and whitespace checks (ADR #47)

Strong player save requirement established: SaveManager must support meta progression saves and run suspend/resume saves with migration, atomic writes, backup fallback, and broken-save quarantine (ADR #48)

Seven creative/strategy subagents added across CodeBuddy, Codex, and OpenCode: game-designer, numeric-designer, ip-designer, copywriter-packager, ui-art-designer, game-art-designer, marketing-strategist (ADR #49)

Communication rule added: when asked for issues/risks, answer factually and do not invent problems; when new requirements arrive, first assess project fit, cost-effectiveness, complexity, and risks (ADR #50)

Data validation, contract sync, and lightweight Godot Bridge landed: tools/sync_contracts.py, tools/validate_data.py, tools/godot_bridge.py, generated _contracts.json and client/scripts/contracts/*.gd, and docs-check CI now runs sync + validate + docs health (ADR #51)
AI Git commit policy established: large changes are auto-committed after status/diff/log checks and verification; minor changes are not auto-committed; only task-owned files may be staged (ADR #52)

Eight project-level skills initially installed under .opencode/skills and now synchronized across .codebuddy/skills, .codex/skills, and .opencode/skills for Godot/GDScript, scene validation, Godot test diagnostics, doc sync, safe git commit, factual code review, AI resource curation, and MCP tool evaluation (ADR #53)

Context-compaction task recovery rule established: after summaries or context restoration, user last explicit instruction overrides summaries, Next Steps, and candidate current_state actions (ADR #54)
Historical external AI resource submodules were installed for review (ADR #55; later superseded by ADR #59)
Historical external AI tools were briefly installed while excluding templates/examples (ADR #56; later superseded by ADR #58 and #59)
Historical CCGS cross-agent adapter was added for on-demand reuse (ADR #57; later superseded by ADR #58 and #59)
Historical external AI library active layer was simplified to a reference adapter (ADR #58; later superseded by ADR #59)

Project-level skills consolidated from vendor/reference into direct project skills: useful GodotPrompter, headless-godot-skill-kit, and CCGS patterns were absorbed; .agents/skills/game-ai-reference, vendor submodules, and .gitmodules were removed (ADR #59)

Nine project-level skills are synchronized across .codebuddy/skills, .codex/skills, and .opencode/skills so CodeBuddy, Codex, and OpenCode can each read direct same-name skills without cross-platform references (ADR #60)

MVP M4.7 added debug/dev_tools-only GM console with stats, HP, damage, spawn, clear/kill, spawner toggle, and reset commands; full project rule requires release presets to exclude debug tools (ADR #61)
User confirmed MVP validation and AI work framework setup are complete; formal full-project roadmap was established in docs/正式项目工作规划.md (ADR #62)
Numeric configuration format policy established: flat table values prefer CSV, complex configuration prefers JSON, and DataLoader should support both (ADR #63)
Initial localization scope established: zh_CN and en are the first two maintained languages, and AI should auto-fill the missing counterpart as a first-draft translation for human review (ADR #64)

Multi-mode resource reuse principle established: resource definitions stay mode-agnostic by default, while future game mode configs compose resource pools, weights, conditions, blocklists, and light overrides (ADR #65)

Future multiplayer PvE/PvP boundary established: do not implement networking now, but avoid hardcoding a single player or fixed player-vs-enemy assumptions across input, Combat, teams, Replay, SaveManager, analytics, and game mode config (ADR #66)
Formal project F1 skeleton established: client/project.godot, minimal boot scene/script, standard client directories, client README, and FormalClientBoot module documentation.
Formal project F2 first autoload slice established: DataLoader, RNG, GameState, and GameClock are registered, boot-smoked, and documented.
Formal project F2 second autoload slice established: Settings, Localization, and UIManager are registered, boot-smoked, and documented.
Formal project F2 Analytics autoload slice established: Analytics is registered, boot-smoked, and documented.
Formal project F2 Replay autoload slice established: Replay is registered, boot-smoked, and documented.
Formal project F2 PoolManager autoload slice established: PoolManager is registered, boot-smoked, and documented.
Formal project F2 SaveManager autoload slice established: SaveManager is registered, boot-smoked, roundtrip-smoked, and documented.
Formal project F2 AudioManager autoload slice established: AudioManager is registered, boot-smoked, and documented.
AI quick-start context path established: docs/AI协作/快速开工.md is the default low-token startup route and long-term memory is cold storage (ADR #67).
F3 DataLoader work package established and code-stage reading now prioritizes module docs plus target source (ADR #68).
Large code changes now require an automatic factual code review before commit, while minor changes do not trigger formal review (ADR #69).

Formal project F3 first DataLoader schema slice established: DataLoader, boot smoke, and tools/validate_data.py validate player.json, meta_progression.json, strings.csv, growth.csv, and growth_pools.json; growth golden examples are documented.
next_actions: Continue F3 through docs/AI协作/工作包/F3-DataLoader.md: add game_modes.json schema boundaries for resource pools, weights, blocklists, lightweight overrides, participants, and teams without implementing mode runtime.
Keep AI context cost low: use stage work packages for formal-project tasks, module docs plus target source for code work, and AGENTS/platform rules as the single red-line authority.

Add GUT or equivalent L1 coverage for DataLoader schema errors plus RNG, GameClock, GameState, Settings, Analytics, Replay, PoolManager, SaveManager, AudioManager, Localization, and UIManager once the project test runner is introduced.
Keep CI stage 1 follow-up on the backlog: commitlint, local pre-commit hook, and incremental watch loop.

last_verified.result: passed; boot log included data_schema_ok=true player_stats=9 locale_keys=25 growth_levels=10 growth_pools=1

last_verified.command: bundled python -m json.tool docs/AI记忆/current_state.json and docs/_kb_index.json

notes: Long-term decisions remain authoritative in docs/决策记录.md.
Use python tools/sync_contracts.py after changing docs/词表与契约.md, then run --check.
Use python tools/validate_data.py after changing client/data, client/locale, or MVP config.
Use python tools/godot_bridge.py for MVP scene-tree export, data validation, Godot version checks, and headless boot.
Do not over-optimize or invent findings when the user asks for issues; say no issue when no actual issue is found.
When the user proposes a requirement, briefly assess project fit, value, cost-effectiveness, complexity, and major risks before implementing.
Detailed daily history remains in docs/AI记忆/会话日志/.
Do not read or modify draft/ or DRAFT/ unless explicitly authorized.

For large AI changes, create a git commit after checking status, diff, recent log, and verification; for large code changes, run factual code review before commit; for minor changes, explain why no commit was made and skip formal review.

Project-level skills live in synchronized .codebuddy/skills, .codex/skills, and .opencode/skills; source/evaluation notes live in docs/AI协作/AI技能资源评估.md; restart/reopen the relevant AI tool after changing skills paths or config.
GM/debug tooling must stay behind debug/dev_tools build gates; release presets must not enable dev_tools or include debug scripts/GM command resources.
External AI resource packages are not kept as vendor submodules; useful external procedures must be absorbed into project-owned skills and project rules remain authoritative.

After context summary, compaction, or restoration, align to the user's last explicit instruction first; summaries, Next Steps, and current_state next_actions are candidate context only, not authorization.
Default AI startup is now the low-token hot path: AGENTS.md -> docs/AI协作/快速开工.md -> current_state.json -> platform rules -> task-relevant AI导航 sections; read long-term memory only when needed.
Formal project stage work should prefer docs/AI协作/工作包/; current F3 entry is docs/AI协作/工作包/F3-DataLoader.md.
Code-stage tasks should read docs/代码文档规范.md, the relevant docs/代码/<module_id>.md, and target source first; read GDD/ADR only for design conflict, unclear semantics, or new decisions.
